name: Sripati Pandita
dates_notes: Dating is disputed between an early Vīraśaiva placement around the twelfth century and later placements around the late fourteenth century.
core_thesis: Śrīpati Paṇḍita is the Vīraśaiva Vedānta author of the *Śrīkarabhāṣya* on the *Brahma-sūtras*. The work is the major Vīraśaiva engagement with the Vedānta sūtra tradition and is reported to argue a Śaiva, Śakti-qualified Vedānta position while refuting rival Vedānta readings, including Rāmānuja. Because the date is genuinely contested and the available text is scan-level rather than a clean local source, this entry records the author, work, affiliation, and broad polemical setting without asserting detailed sūtra-level doctrine.

engaged_works.title: Srikara-bhasya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Standardly attributed to Śrīpati Paṇḍita in the Vīraśaiva Vedānta tradition; the author's date remains disputed.
engaged_works.summary: A Vīraśaiva commentary on the *Brahma-sūtras* and the principal textual basis for Śrīpati Paṇḍita's Śaiva Vedānta. It is important because it places Vīraśaiva theology into direct conversation with the pan-Vedānta sūtra-commentary format and argues against rival Vedānta positions. No clean local text has been acquired; the gap report notes scan availability.
